up.method: Ethanol substrate; max acetaldehyde at 2000 mM ethanol; C1/2 (half-max) = 38.8 +/- 15.4 mM. No molar ethanol uptake rate measured

sec.method: Vmax by sensor gas chromatography on resting washed cells at 2000 mM ethanol; +/- 12.7

growth_records.cite: Tagaino R et al. Bifacial biological effects of ethanol: acetaldehyde production by oral Streptococcus species and the antibacterial effects of ethanol against these bacteria. Journal of Oral Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.1080/20002297.2021.1937884. [PMC8204988]
growth_records.snippet: The maximum acetaldehyde production (Vmax) (umol/mg of wet weight of cells/min) S. mitis 72.1 +/- 12.7 ... C1/2 38.8 +/- 15.4 mM.
growth_records.notes: Resting-cell (non-growing) enzymatic assay, NOT growth-phase flux. Acetaldehyde Vmax=72.1 umol/mg wet wt/min at 2000 mM ethanol (Table 1). C1/2=38.8 mM (lowest of the 5 strains -> produces acetaldehyde at low ethanol). Growth doubling times vs ethanol shown only in Figure 1 (no numeric control value in text); growth inhibited >=1000 mM, no growth >=2000 mM ethanol. Ethanol is metabolic substrate via ADH+NADH oxidase.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 14734 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/14734
